name: pykdumpfile

metadata.name: pykdumpfile
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 or later (GPLv2+)

documentation:
# pykdumpfile

Bindings to the libkdumpfile shared library.

## Links

* [libkdumpfile](https://github.com/ptesarik/libkdumpfile)

## License

This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under
the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software
Foundation; either version 2 of the License, or (at your option) any later
version.

functions.name: addrxlat.VER_LINUX
documentation:
VER_LINUX(a, b, c) -> version code

Calculate the Linux kernel version code.
## Version codes

functions.name: addrxlat.VER_XEN
documentation:
VER_XEN(major, minor) -> version code

Calculate the Xen hypervisor version code.

functions.name: addrxlat.addrspace_name
documentation:
addrspace_name(addrspace) -> name

Return the name of an address space constant.
## Other static functions

functions.name: addrxlat.CAPS
documentation:
CAPS(addrspace) -> capability bitmask

Translate an address space constant into a capability bitmask.

functions.name: addrxlat.pte_format_name
documentation:
pte_format_name(fmt) -> name

Return the name of a PTE format constant.

functions.name: addrxlat.pte_format
documentation:
pte_format(name) -> fmt

Return the PTE format constant by name.

functions.name: addrxlat.pteval_shift
documentation:
pteval_shift(fmt) -> bit shift

Get the pteval shift for a PTE format.
See PTE_xxx for valid values of fmt.

documentation:
Failed to index ``addrxlat/defs_py2.py`` due to ``Syntax Error @ 8:1.
parser error: error at 8:15: expected one of !=, %, &, (, *, **, +, -, ., /, //, ;, <, <<, <=, ==, >, >=, >>, @, NEWLINE, [, ^, and, from, if, in, is, not, or, |

    raise exc, val, tb
^``

modules.name: addrxlat.exceptions
documentation: addrxlat.exceptions

functions.name: addrxlat.exceptions.strerror
documentation:
strerror(status) -> error message

Return the string describing a given error status.

functions.name: addrxlat.exceptions._def_exception
documentation:
Create an addrxlat exception for a given status code.
The new exception is derived from AddrxlatError, but you may specify
additional base classes with addbases.

functions.name: addrxlat.exceptions.get_exception
documentation:
get_exception(status[, message])

Get an appropriate exception for the given status. If there is no
specific exception, make an instance of AddrxlatError.

classes.name: addrxlat.exceptions.AddrxlatError
documentation:
Common base for all addrxlat exceptions.

AddrxlatError(status[, message])

If message is not specified, use addrxlat_strerror(status).

Attributes:
  status   addrxlat status code, see ERR_xxx
  message  verbose error message

functions.name: kdumpfile.exceptions._def_exception
documentation:
Create an kdumpfile exception for a given status code.
The new exception is derived from KdumpfileError, but you may specify

documentation:
get_exception(status[, message])

Get an appropriate exception for the given status. If there is no

classes.name: kdumpfile.exceptions.KdumpfileError
documentation:
Common base for all kdumpfile exceptions.

KdumpfileError(status[, message])

If message is not specified, use kdumpfile_strerror(status).

Attributes:
  status   kdumpfile status code, see ERR_xxx
  message  verbose error message

modules.name: addrxlat.objects

addrxlat.objects

There are two types of objects in libaddrxlat:

- simple value
- reference-counted

CTX.cb_get_page(buf)

Callback function to read a page at a given address. Update the Buffer

documentation:
CTX.next_cb_get_page(buf)

Call the next callback to read a page.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.Context.cb_read_caps
documentation:
CTX.cb_read_caps() -> read_caps

Callback function to get a bitmask of address spaces accepted by
CTX.cb_read_page(). Returns CTX.read_caps if not None, otherwise
calls CTX.next_cb_read_caps().

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.Context.next_cb_read_caps
documentation:
CTX.next_cb_read_caps()

Call the next read capabilities callback.

Context([ptr]) -> ctx

Wrapper for cdata addrxlat_ctx_t *.
If ptr is omitted, allocate a new translation context.
## addrxlat_ctx_t

classes.name: addrxlat.objects.Method

documentation: translation kind

documentation: target address space

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.Method.param
documentation: method parameters as a raw byte buffer

Method(kind) -> address translation method

This is a generic base class for all translation desriptions.
Use a subclass to get a more suitable interface to the parameters
of a specific translation kind.
## addrxlat_meth_t

classes.name: addrxlat.objects.CustomMethod

documentation: raw first_step callback

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.CustomMethod.cb_first_step
documentation:
METH.cb_first_step(step, addr)

Callback to perform the initial translation step.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.CustomMethod._cb_next_step
documentation: raw next_step callback

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.CustomMethod.cb_next_step
documentation:
METH.cb_next_step(step)

Callback to perform further translation steps.

CustomMethod() -> custom address translation method

This is an abstract base class for translation methods based on
callback. Change the cb_first_step and cb_next_step methods to
get a working instance.
ADDRXLAT_CUSTOM

classes.name: addrxlat.objects.ForwardCustomMethod

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.ForwardCustomMethod.cb_first_step
documentation: Forward the first step callback to the wrapped method.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.ForwardCustomMethod.cb_next_step
documentation: Forward the next step callback to the wrapped method.

ForwardCustomMethod(ptr) -> custom address translation method.

Wrapper around an existing custom translation method. Callbacks are
forwarded to that object's implementation. Instances of this class
are created by MethodFromCData.

classes.name: addrxlat.objects.Map

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.Map.set
documentation:
MAP.set(addr, range) -> status

Modify map so that addresses between addr and addr+range.off

documentation:
MAP.search(addr) -> meth

Find the translation method for the given address.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.Map.copy
documentation:
M.copy() -> map

Return a shallow copy of a translation map.

Map([ptr]) -> map

Wrapper for cdata addrxlat_map_t *.
If ptr is omitted, allocate a new translation map.

classes.name: addrxlat.objects.System

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.System.os_init
documentation:
SYS.os_init(...) -> status

Set up a translation system for a pre-defined operating system.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.System.set_map
documentation:
SYS.set_map(idx, map)

Explicitly set the given translation map of a translation system.
See SYS_MAP_xxx for valid values of idx.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.System.get_map
documentation:
SYS.get_map(idx) -> Map or None

Get the given translation map of a translation system.
See SYS_MAP_xxx for valid values of idx.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.System.set_meth
documentation:
SYS.set_meth(idx, meth)

Explicitly set a pre-defined translation method of a translation
system.
See SYS_METH_xxx for valid values of idx.

methods.name: addrxlat.objects.System.get_meth
documentation:
SYS.get_meth(idx) -> Method

Get the given translation method of a translation system.
See SYS_METH_xxx for valid values of idx.

SystemBase([ptr]) -> sys

Wrapper for cdata addrxlat_sys_t *.
If ptr is omitted, allocate a new translation system.

variables.name: tests.test_addrxlat.CUSTOM_MAGIC_ADDR
documentation: My_Magic

variables.name: tests.test_addrxlat.CUSTOM_MAGIC_ADDR2
documentation: MagicToo

classes.name: tests.test_addrxlat.TestTranslation
documentation: Test translation system has the following layout:
MAP_HW:
0-ffff: PGT root=MACHPHYSADDR:0
10000-ffffffffffffffff: NONE
MAP_KV_PHYS:
0-1fff: DIRECT off=0x1000
2000-3fff: LOOKUP
4000-5fff: MEMARR base=KVADDR:0
6000-ffff: PGT
10000-ffffffffffffffff: NONE
MAP_KPHYS_DIRECT:
0-fff: NONE
1000-2fff: RDIRECT
3000-ffffffffffffffff: NONE
MAP_MACHPHYS_KPHYS:
0-ffff: NONE
10000-1ffff: KPHYS_MACHPHYS
20000-ffffffffffffffff: NONE
MAP_KPHYS_MACHPHYS:
0-ffff: MACHPHYS_KPHYS
10000-ffffffffffffffff: NONE
